name: Sheppard AFB
tagline: The Air Force's largest training base, home of technical training and the multinational ENJJPT pilot program, in north Texas.
_note: Air Force training base (Wichita Falls, north TX; Central time). CONUS full template (BAH MHA TX288 Wichita Falls/Sheppard AFB; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). 82nd Training Wing (technical training) + 80th Flying Training Wing (ENJJPT, the only multinational pilot training program). Medical: 82nd Medical Group clinic. Facts verified from official sources (sheppard.af.mil, 82 MDG/TRICARE, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Texas moved to TRICARE West in 2024. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 76308 (Wichita Falls).

data.Location: Wichita Falls, TX (north Texas, near the Oklahoma line)
data.Role: The Air Force's largest and most diversified training base
data.Major units: 82nd Training Wing (technical training) and 80th Flying Training Wing
data.Notable: Home of ENJJPT, the only multinational (NATO) jet pilot training program

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Sheppard Air Force Base is the Air Force's largest and most diversified training base. The 82nd Training Wing runs a huge range of technical training, while the 80th Flying Training Wing hosts the Euro-NATO Joint Jet Pilot Training program (ENJJPT), the world's only multinational pilot training program.
In Wichita Falls in north Texas, the base has a constant flow of students and instructors and an affordable cost of living, with an international flavor thanks to ENJJPT's partner nations.

data.subtitle: Wichita Falls is an affordable, manageable-size city. The main choice is on-base housing versus the local neighborhoods, with a short commute either way.
intro: Wichita Falls has a low cost of living, so BAH stretches well, and the city is small enough that commutes are short wherever you land. On-base housing is available; many also live off base.
Where you live mostly comes down to schools and preference across Wichita Falls and nearby Burkburnett, with the DFW metro a couple of hours southeast for bigger trips.

recommendations.advice: Northern Wichita Falls and Burkburnett sit near the base for a short commute.
Wichita Falls and Burkburnett offer affordable family neighborhoods and schools.
On-base housing is available and convenient, popular given the training-heavy population.
The area is very affordable, so BAH tends to go a long way.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Sheppard AFB site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Sheppard AFB has privatized on-base family housing across several neighborhoods.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA TX288 (Wichita Falls/Sheppard AFB)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Wichita Falls ISD serves the city, and nearby Burkburnett ISD serves families on the north side near the base.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Outpatient primary and some specialty care on base; inpatient, emergency, and complex care via Wichita Falls-area network hospitals

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and Wichita Falls' very affordable market makes the call mostly about your tour length.
rent: Your tour is short or you're here for a training pipeline.
You want time to learn the area before committing.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing in a smaller market.
You prefer on-base housing for the short commute and convenience.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years (often instructor tours).
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The very affordable Wichita Falls market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. Wichita Falls is inexpensive, so BAH stretches well and buying can pencil out on a longer tour, though the smaller market affects resale. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the housing office's read on Wichita Falls and Burkburnett before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Texas vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (Wichita Falls ISD or Burkburnett ISD) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at the 82nd Medical Group clinic or network providers.

data.q: What is Sheppard AFB known for?
data.a: It's the Air Force's largest and most diversified training base, home to wide-ranging technical training (82nd Training Wing) and ENJJPT, the only multinational NATO jet pilot training program (80th Flying Training Wing).
On-base housing is available; many live off base in Wichita Falls or nearby Burkburnett. Commutes are short either way.
Very. Wichita Falls has a low cost of living, so BAH stretches well.
Sheppard is MHA TX288 (Wichita Falls/Sheppard AFB). Use the calculator above for your grade.
